question: Which local Pixie affordances produce visible, bounded, persistent, and substrate-specific responses in three deterministic physics-native critters?
experimental_unit: one independently seeded paired treated-versus-observe sanctuary episode

hypotheses.prediction: Each critter's taxonomy-preferred action produces a visible bounded response in at least 75% of confirmatory and holdout episodes, while observe produces exactly zero divergence.
hypotheses.falsifier: Any preferred action falls below 75% visible bounded response on either fresh split, or any observe episode diverges from its paired baseline.

hypotheses.prediction: At least two of the three preferred critter/action pairings retain a tail divergence of at least 0.003 in at least 75% of confirmatory and holdout episodes.
hypotheses.falsifier: Fewer than two preferred pairings meet the frozen tail-divergence occupancy on either fresh split.

hypotheses.prediction: The preferred-action response-class distribution depends on critter substrate, with mutual information above 0.10 bits on confirmatory and holdout splits.
hypotheses.falsifier: Family/response-class mutual information is at most 0.10 bits on either fresh split.

hypotheses.prediction: No preferred-action episode crosses the frozen globalized-response hazard threshold of 0.75 divergent sites.
hypotheses.falsifier: Any confirmatory or holdout preferred-action episode globalizes.

model.update_order: At declared ticks the Pixie action is applied to the treated state, then treated and paired observe worlds receive the same synchronous substrate update; shield restores its local pre-update state for four ticks.
model.rng: numpy.default_rng(seed) initializes paired worlds identically. No randomness is consumed after initialization; action schedules and updates are deterministic.

seed_plan.pairing: Treated and observe comparator are exact copies at initialization; no post-initialization RNG exists, so their only difference is the declared action coupling.

denominators.action_execution_rate: executed scheduled actions divided by three scheduled action opportunities
denominators.divergent_fraction: treated-versus-paired-observe differing categorical sites divided by all world sites
denominators.response_localization: divergent sites within Manhattan radius six of the Pixie divided by all divergent sites
denominators.preferred_action_occupancy: independently seeded episodes for the declared critter/action/split cell

exposure.eligibility: Every scheduled action targets a nonempty periodic mask centered on the Pixie.
exposure.activation: Every action condition emits exactly three attempts at ticks 8, 16, and 24; observe emits receipts but requests no state change.
exposure.state_change: Non-observe actions retain exact changed-site counts; shield execution counts update differences prevented inside its active mask.

analysis.uncertainty: Episode-level Jeffreys intervals for cell occupancy and deterministic episode bootstrap intervals for continuous response metrics; ticks and sites are repeated measurements.
analysis.multiple_comparisons: All 18 critter/action cells are retained. Preferred actions and thresholds are frozen in the taxonomy before discovery; no champion is selected from the matrix.
analysis.stopping_rule: Stop after all 162 declared episodes, at 300 wall seconds, above 1024 MB process RSS, above 100 MB retained artifacts, or on failed exact replay.

budget.execution: Sequential local CPU execution; paired worlds run in one process with no worker parallelism or external model calls.
